office_does: State representatives write and vote on state laws and budgets, oversee state government, and represent their legislative district.
race_blurb: Incumbent Joel McEntire faces educator Daniel William Bradley, natural-resources worker Jimi O'Hagan, and labor leader Terry Carlson.

candidates.summary: Bradley is an educator and public employee running on traceable spending, measurable results, and taxpayer protection.
highlights: Calls for transparent and traceable public spending [S1]
Emphasizes measurable results and taxpayer protection [S1]

spending.basis: His campaign centers on tracing spending and demanding measurable results before more expenditure.

experience.basis: He brings education and public-work experience but no documented elected record.

candidates.summary: O'Hagan has farming, fishing, forestry, and construction experience and calls for investigations into alleged official corruption.
highlights: Calls for legislative and grand-jury investigations of alleged corruption [S1]
The dossier notes those allegations were not independently substantiated [S1]

reform.basis: His focus on investigating official corruption supplies an accountability direction, but no broader structural reform is specified.

experience.basis: His professional background is outside elected government.

candidates.summary: McEntire is the incumbent representative, Marine veteran, and educator who emphasizes fiscal restraint, lower taxes, business growth, and infrastructure.
highlights: Supports limiting taxes and restraining spending [S1][S2]

taxes.basis: He explicitly supports limiting taxes and criticizes recent tax increases.

spending.basis: Fiscal restraint and insufficient benefit from recent spending are central stated positions.

experience.basis: He is an incumbent with accessible voting, committee, and sponsorship records.

candidates.summary: Carlson is a longtime timber worker and labor leader who emphasizes affordability, schools, workers, families, and accountable leadership.
highlights: Leads regional and local labor organizations [S1]
Emphasizes affordability, economic growth, schools, workers, families, and seniors [S1]

experience.basis: He brings substantial labor leadership but no documented elected or legislative record.
